frameworks.name: recurrent network model of primary visual cortex
frameworks.one_line: Recurrent V1 dynamics drive short-timescale adaptation effects

frameworks.name: tilt aftereffect
frameworks.one_line: Classic repulsive TAE extended to attractive short-timescale regime

core_question: Can recurrent V1 network dynamics produce an attractive tilt aftereffect on short timescales?
key_assumptions: Short- and long-timescale adaptation can have different signs
Behavioral aftereffects reflect underlying V1 population dynamics
manipulations: Adapter-to-test time scale (short ~100 ms vs longer)
Novel adaptation protocol targeting short-timescale exposure
key_findings: Model predicted an attractive TAE on the ~100 ms timescale
Behavioral experiments confirmed the attractive short-term TAE
interpretations: Recurrent network dynamics contribute substantially to short-term adaptation
TAE sign depends on timescale and underlying network mechanism
